CREATE TABLE timestamps (event_time TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
SELECT event_time, make_timestamptz(2000, 1, 15, 8, 30, 15.678) AS new_time FROM timestamps WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE events (id INTEGER, start_time TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, end_time TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);
INSERT INTO events VALUES (1, make_timestamptz(2021, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0.0), make_timestamptz(2021, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0.0));
INSERT INTO events VALUES (2, make_timestamptz(2021, 1, 2, 11, 30, 0.0), make_timestamptz(2021, 1, 2, 14, 30, 0.0));
SELECT e.id, e.start_time, e.end_time, (e.end_time - e.start_time) AS duration FROM events e WHERE e.start_time < make_timestamptz(2021, 1, 3, 0, 0, 0.0);
SELECT * FROM timestamps WHERE event_time BETWEEN make_timestamptz(1990, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0.0) AND make_timestamptz(2030, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59.999999);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE (end_time - start_time) > INTERVAL '2 hours';
WITH ts AS (SELECT make_timestamptz(2022, 5, 21, 15, 0, 0.0) AS ts1, make_timestamptz(2022, 5, 22, 16, 30, 45.0) AS ts2) SELECT (ts1 - ts2) AS time_difference FROM ts;
CREATE TABLE date_ranges AS SELECT make_timestamptz(2023, 10, 1, 0, 0, 0.0) AS start, make_timestamptz(2023, 10, 31, 23, 59, 59.999999) AS end;
SELECT * FROM date_ranges WHERE start < make_timestamptz(2023, 10, 15, 0, 0, 0.0) AND end > make_timestamptz(2023, 10, 15, 23, 59, 59.0);
